Event: Annie decides to get an abortion because she and Hughie feel unready and fear Homelander’s world
Explanation: She avoids bringing a child into a highly dangerous environment but suffers deep trauma and guilt with no broader good achieved.

Event: Sister Sage conspires to have Firecracker publicly reveal Annie’s abortion
Explanation: Sage advances her anti-Annie political strategy by inflicting massive reputational and psychological harm on Annie.

Event: Firecracker reveals Annie’s abortion on live television, demonizing her
Explanation: She boosts her profile and cause by humiliating Annie and shattering her public image.

Event: Annie savagely beats Firecracker onstage after the abortion reveal
Explanation: She vents rage but nearly kills Firecracker on live TV, losing allies, public support, and political leverage.

Event: Butcher ends his deal with Annie after seeing her attack on Firecracker
Explanation: He protects his political position and bill optics at Annie’s expense, withdrawing crucial institutional support from her.

Event: Annie accidentally blinds a woman in her first rescue at age 13
Explanation: Her attempt to save hostages succeeds but catastrophically harms an innocent due to uncontrolled power.

Event: Firecracker later publicizes Annie’s childhood blinding incident to smear her
Explanation: She gains propaganda ammo while gratuitously dragging an old victimizing mistake into the spotlight against Annie.

Event: Annie accidentally kills a bystander who aims a gun at her and Butcher
Explanation: She and Hughie survive immediately, but she kills a mistaken civilian and emotionally numbs herself, deepening her moral damage.

Event: Starlight “soft” blackmails Stillwell using Vought’s #MeToo optics to avoid being fired
Explanation: She preserves her Seven position and leverage while forcing Vought to respect her assault allegation optics.

Event: Annie secretly films Gecko selling his limb and blackmails him to steal Compound V
Explanation: She gains critical intel for the Boys but coerces and endangers a former friend.

Event: Annie uses the Gecko tape to blackmail A-Train into cooperation
Explanation: She weakens a dangerous supe and aids the Boys via coercion that could destroy his life.

Event: Annie leaks Compound V to the press as a mole
Explanation: She meaningfully damages Vought and exposes corruption but also destabilizes the supe world and her own safety.

Event: Annie beats up a thug harassing a girl in her establishing moment
Explanation: She protects a victim, asserts her heroic identity, and faces little downside.

Event: Annie joins the Boys as a mole inside the Seven
Explanation: She gains allies and purpose while supplying them insider intel against Vought.

Event: Hughie starts taking V24 to get powers and “protect” Annie
Explanation: He gains temporary power and pride but risks severe health damage and strains his relationship.

Event: Hughie stops using V24 and chooses to help Starlight without powers
Explanation: He protects his health, restores trust with Annie, and still contributes meaningfully.

Event: Homelander forces Annie into a fake public relationship as punishment
Explanation: He increases control and PR narrative while inflicting ongoing humiliation and fear on Annie.

Event: Annie initiates the kiss with Homelander onstage to manage the situation
Explanation: She strategically leans into the façade to buy time and reduce Homelander’s suspicion, despite personal disgust.

Event: Homelander kills Supersonic to punish Annie’s coup plotting
Explanation: He consolidates control and terrorizes Annie at the cost of an ally’s life.

Event: Annie publicly exposes Homelander and Vought at the end of Herogasm and quits
Explanation: She reclaims integrity and inspires resistance but loses institutional protection and becomes a top target.

Event: Annie quits the Seven and throws away her Starlight costume
Explanation: She abandons a toxic role while aligning with her real values and new allies.

Event: Annie beats The Deep unconscious after A-Train’s intervention
Explanation: She neutralizes a dangerous abuser with minimal visible fallout, regaining agency and some deterrence.

Event: Annie kills the shapeshifter supe who kidnapped and impersonated her
Explanation: She ends an immediate lethal threat, rescues others, and reasserts control over her identity.

Event: Annie defeats Black Noir II using pure skill without powers
Explanation: She proves her combat growth, removes a major threat, and gains no evident downside.

Event: Annie establishes and runs Starlight House for homeless/at-risk youth
Explanation: She leverages her brand to create concrete material help for vulnerable children.

Event: Annie uses her popularity to revert to her original, less sexualized costume
Explanation: She regains bodily autonomy, shifts her image toward genuine heroism, and still remains marketable.

Event: Hughie joins the Seven despite Annie warning him it’s dangerous
Explanation: He gets proximity to Vought and Annie but exposes himself to lethal supe politics.

Event: Annie calls out Butcher’s supe racism and compares him to Homelander
Explanation: She risks his ire but confronts a destructive bias that undermines their alliance.

Event: Annie lashes out at Hughie over the shapeshifter incident, blaming him
Explanation: She gains no benefit and damages trust while he acted under deception; her trauma drives needless hurt.

Event: Annie lies to Butcher about knowing why Firecracker hates her
Explanation: She momentarily avoids shame but convinces no one and weakens trust with a key ally.

Event: Annie spreads false rumors as a teen that Sparkler/Firecracker slept with judges
Explanation: She boosts her pageant success at the cost of Firecracker’s reputation and livelihood.

Event: Annie later sincerely apologizes to Firecracker, accepting that her cruelty was part of her
Explanation: She gains self-knowledge and offers genuine remorse, slightly softening Firecracker’s hostility.

Event: Annie comforts Frenchie about his guilt and suggests he talk to someone (Kimiko)
Explanation: She deepens trust and steers him toward healthier coping with no visible downside.

Event: Hughie keeps Annie in the dark about working with Soldier Boy and V24
Explanation: He briefly avoids conflict but corrodes trust and endangers coordination against Homelander.

Event: Annie returns to heavy drinking when learning about being dosed with Compound V as a baby
Explanation: She numbs the pain short-term while worsening her mental state and self-control.

Event: Maeve confronts Annie about “selling out,” prompting Annie to push back with her idolization story
Explanation: She jolts Maeve into self-reflection and partially reignites her buried idealism.

Event: Annie hyperventilates and breaks down on discovering her parents agreed to dose her with V
Explanation: She suffers a crisis that ultimately frees her from parental manipulation but shatters her identity.

Event: Annie blackmails Vought by pointing out firing a public assault victim would tank their stock
Explanation: She protects her position and forces the company to align, however cynically, with her narrative.

Event: Annie attempts to reform Vought from within as co-captain
Explanation: Her good-faith effort barely changes anything and burns her out, though it buys some time against Homelander.

Event: Homelander frames Annie as a child trafficker and pedophile through her foundation
Explanation: He redirects public anger and further demonizes Annie to secure his power.

Event: Firecracker brands Annie as the Antichrist through numerology and conspiracy
Explanation: They mobilize a base and sabotage anti-supe efforts via smear, deeply harming Annie and the truth.

Event: Sister Sage gives Firecracker Annie’s private medical and childhood info to trigger her
Explanation: She weaponizes Annie’s trauma to provoke a meltdown that strategically discredits Annie.

Event: Annie violently punches Victoria Neuman after Victoria calls out her emotional loss of control
Explanation: She proves Neuman’s point, gains nothing, and risks antagonizing a powerful figure.

Event: Annie rescues Hughie, Frenchie, Kimiko, and MM from Vought forces in S1 finale
Explanation: She fully embraces being a hero, saving allies at some risk but with massive upside for their cause.

Event: Annie rescues President-elect Singer, Hughie, and MM from the shapeshifter assassin
Explanation: She prevents major political and personal assassinations, greatly aiding the anti-Homelander struggle.

Event: Annie records Homelander’s confession over a livestream by tricking him
Explanation: She risks death but secures powerful evidence that undermines his image.

Event: Annie chooses to help the injured at Herogasm instead of joining the fight
Explanation: She maximizes lives saved while others handle the direct combat.

Event: Annie supports using the Supe-killing virus even at cost of her own life
Explanation: She accepts self-sacrifice and mass killing to protect humanity, bearing heavy moral and personal cost.

Event: Annie helps lead La Résistance against Homelander and Vought
Explanation: She becomes a focal point for organized opposition despite the personal toll.

Event: Annie delays rescuing Hughie from the camps for nearly a year out of fear and avoidance
Explanation: She spares herself facing guilt in the short term but leaves him imprisoned and strains their bond.

Event: Annie chooses to prioritize freeing Frenchie over Hughie because Frenchie can make the virus
Explanation: She adopts a cold utilitarian choice that helps the broader mission but harms her relationship and psyche.

Event: Billy calls Annie out for not rescuing Hughie and tells her to let go of guilt
Explanation: His bluntness hurts but helps her confront avoidance and re-engage with the fight.

Event: Annie snaps Cindy’s neck when she thinks Cindy killed Hughie
Explanation: She eliminates a powerful supe but acts in blind rage on a misperception, deepening her moral slide.

Event: Annie leaves the team after believing Hughie dead becomes too much to bear
Explanation: She reduces her emotional pain but abandons allies when her leadership is needed most.
